description: The distance from the top or surface to the bottom; the quality of being deep. 'Nusho' names vertical extent downward—how far something goes beneath, how profound something reaches.
sound_symbolism: The word descends. 'Nu' grounds with downward pull, depth beginning. Then 'sho' /ʃo/ continues into the deep, extent measured.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo nusho' (the depth). Related to adjective 'nulo' (deep). Opposite dimension from 'raeli' (height).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Depth of practice penetrates surface—going beneath habit to understanding, beneath reaction to response.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Depth determined water—wells, lakes, ocean all measured by how far down they extended.
